Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and low serum calcium. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease.
